@peirce_alva @haydendevs The man stood his ground.
@SwiftBovine @TheOnion Normally I'd agree with you, but I might not quite be willing to die for the right to distribute depictions of animal cruelty.
@grammarengli "Wi-Fi" has no full form. It's a brand name that neither means nor stands for anything. This has been confirmed by Phil Belanger, founder and first chairman of the Wi-Fi Alliance.
@SwiftBovine @TheOnion Distributing depictions of animal cruelty is protected.

I have no words for how evil this is. I'm literally in shock. Everyone involved in producing and distributing this needs to examine all their life choices that led them to such a repulsive display. https://t.co/B5uftAd4jV

@haydendevs So you can decide to block the author so they can't use your reach to get to your followers.
Protected by the First Amendment. Really! https://t.co/BBzJcNxyma
@benrayfield I don't understand what you think that means. You do not need any licence to download, install, and use Linux. None of those things are rights reserved to a copyright holder,

@Daveinthenorth2 @ronrule This is incredibly silly. Say I have liquid asset that is now worth $50. I can trade that paper wealth for $50 and someone with $50 can trade that $50 for the same paper wealth I had. To say one is somehow really wealth and the other somehow isn't is utterly absurd.
@RandomSprint Where is 12?!
@Delrio2024 All my friends are just out of frame. You wouldn't know them, they go to a different school.

@thefrozenfire @jimmy_wales @prestonjbyrne We do have something similar, but we are fortunate that our Supreme Court really holds the government to all the prongs of the strict scrutiny test. In fact, I can't think of a single case where the Supreme Court has held that the test was satisfied.
@PeterSMagnusson @TheVixhal Then picking a random number between zero and one is meaningless too, since there's a one-to-one correspondence between a number and its reciprocal.

@rolandbouman @a_cuniculturist @russellbrandom We have never held speakers liable for how mentally unstable people might irrationally react to their speech. If we did, the movie industry would have to change radically, if it could even exist.

probability.last_reasoning: Slight increase in bear probability from 10% to 12% driven by: (1) accelerating geopolitical risk with Iran/Hormuz/China fuel export suspension creating real commodity shock scenario, (2) departure of key Senate crypto legislative champions weakening Clarity Act pathway, (3) a16z fund size halving signals possible VC appetite cooling. Base case narrowed slightly from 40% to 42% as RLUSD recovery and SEC guidance provide near-term support. Mid case reduced from 38% to 34% reflecting that macro deterioration makes aggressive upside scenarios harder to achieve in compressed timeframes. Bull unchanged at 12% — the structural thesis drivers (ETFs, RLUSD, infrastructure) remain intact but macro headwinds are intensifying.

bear_case.score_reasoning: Bear case pressure elevated from prior cycles. Three drivers: (1) PermissionedDEX live 13 days with zero institutional adoption — silence is data, (2) a16z fund halving signals VC crypto appetite cooling, (3) macro stress intensifying with Iran escalation could trigger sustained risk-off that delays institutional deployment timelines regardless of infrastructure readiness.

competing_infrastructure.name: SWIFT GPI
competing_infrastructure.status_text: SWIFT continues incremental upgrades to 11,500-bank network. No dramatic new capability but persistent improvement reduces urgency for alternatives. The 'good enough' problem remains the strongest quiet threat.

competing_infrastructure.name: Visa B2B Connect
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Visa B2B Connect expanding but focused on different market segment (corporate payments vs institutional settlement). Not a direct competitor to XRPL bridge asset thesis.

competing_infrastructure.name: JPMorgan Kinexys
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Kinexys (formerly Onyx) continues to operate JPM Coin for institutional clients. Closed-loop system but demonstrates that major banks prefer proprietary settlement rails. Bank preference for internal systems is a structural headwind.

competing_infrastructure.name: BIS Project Nexus
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Nexus designed to link domestic instant payment systems without requiring any bridge asset or private token. If successful across 5+ jurisdictions, it directly undermines the core value proposition of XRP as a bridge asset. Most credible systemic competitor.

competing_infrastructure.name: Ethereum Institutional
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Ethereum L2 ecosystem continuing to develop institutional applications. BlackRock BUIDL fund on Ethereum demonstrates institutional preference for ETH-based tokenization. However, Ethereum's settlement finality and throughput characteristics differ from XRPL's design target.

bear_case.odl_stagnation: ODL volume data has been unavailable for extended period — the lack of transparency itself is a bear signal, as Ripple would presumably publish strong numbers if they existed.
bear_case.token_velocity_concern: No token velocity data available. If XRP is used purely as a bridge asset with sub-second hold times, value accrual to token holders is minimal regardless of volume — this remains the most underappreciated structural risk to the investment thesis.

macro_headwinds.name: Global Recession Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: South Korea KOSPI -20% in two days, Iran war escalating, China restricting fuel exports, Fear & Greed at 22. Regional instability could cascade. Oil shock path to global recession is now plausible, not hypothetical.

macro_headwinds.name: Crypto Winter Signals
macro_headwinds.status_text: Fear & Greed at 22 (extreme fear), a16z fund halving, XRP -55% from cycle highs. Not yet a crypto winter but early warning indicators are accumulating. Extended macro stress could trigger sustained institutional capital withdrawal.

macro_headwinds.name: Regulatory Reversal Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: SEC interpretive guidance is positive but departure of key Senate crypto champions (Lummis, Daines, Blackburn) weakens legislative durability. Executive action can be reversed by future administrations. Legislative codification path is narrowing.

macro_headwinds.name: Rate Hike Extension
macro_headwinds.status_text: US 10Y at 4.06% is stable. Fed appears to be in extended hold mode rather than hiking. Oil shock could complicate inflation trajectory but not yet triggering rate hike expectations.

bear_case.bear_narrative: HOLD POSITION with increased monitoring. The thesis is structurally intact — 7 ETFs, SBI resource commitment, XRP defying extreme fear at +2.59% — but operationally unverifiable on its most important dimension: bridge settlement volume has been dark for 2.5 years. Two kill switches elevated to WARNING (ODL Volume, ODL Transparency) and one trending toward failure (RLUSD at 32% needing 3-4x current pace). Compound stress is pre-loaded with all three legs approaching thresholds simultaneously; China's diesel export suspension signals preparation for extended Middle East disruption that could cascade Brent past $85 within days, pushing stress to ELEVATED. The paradox holds: the macro stress that could crash the position is the same stress that would prove the thesis — fragile settlement infrastructure fails precisely when it's needed most. Bear pressure at 38/100: moderate, driven primarily by the data vacuum, not by structural thesis failure. What we don't know (ODL volume, ETF AUM) matters more than what we do know.
